sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Intel’s chief people officer: Humans need to be at the center of AI
feed.summary: Intel's chief people officer, Christy Pambianchi, emphasizes the importance of keeping humans at the center of artificial intelligence development and integration. The company, an early pioneer in microprocessors, is now deeply invested in AI through initiatives like its AI Inside program, proprietary iGPT, and a significant AI upskilling program. Pambianchi explains how Intel plans to responsibly incorporate AI into its workforce, encourage employee adoption, and prepare staff for future AI-driven roles.

feed.title: Applied Digital to sell 200MW Garden City data center campus to Marathon Digital
feed.summary: Applied Digital is selling its 200MW Garden City data center campus in Texas to Marathon Digital Holdings for $87.3 million. This strategic move aims to pivot Applied Digital away from cryptomining hosting and towards high-performance computing (HPC) and AI customers, leveraging the sale to bolster its balance sheet and fund HPC infrastructure development. Marathon Digital, already a significant customer, will take over the facility, highlighting a shift in Applied Digital's business focus.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. stock underperforms Monday when compared to competitors
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) stock fell by 0.21% on Monday, closing at $190.65, despite a positive trading session for the broader stock market. The S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average both saw gains. AMD's stock closed $36.65 below its 52-week high of $227.30, which it reached on March 8th.

feed.title: AMD Showcases Networking Security Innovations, 800G Ethernet Interoperability at OFC 2024
feed.summary: AMD will showcase its latest networking security innovations and 800G Ethernet interoperability at OFC 2024. The demonstrations include 800G Ethernet using Versal Premium devices, AI-inferencing for DDoS mitigation, and 100Gbps TLS-1.3 offload for enhanced network security. These advancements aim to address the increasing demand for network performance and security in core network infrastructures and edge nodes.

feed.title: Gallium Nitride Market Size 2024 Industry Analysis, Key Players, Regional Demand, Revenue, Opportunity and Forecast 2032
feed.summary: The global gallium nitride (GaN) market is projected to reach US$ 298 million by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 9.5% from 2021 to 2030, driven by its superior properties for electronic devices, energy efficiency, and applications in hybrid electric vehicles and 5G infrastructure. North America leads the market, while Asia Pacific, particularly China, shows substantial growth due to R&D and technological advancements. Key players include Cree Inc., Infineon Technologies AG, and GaN Systems Inc.

feed.title: AMDL ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns NASDAQ:AMDL
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of the GraniteShares 2x Long AMD Daily ETF (AMDL), an ETF designed as a short-term tactical tool to deliver twice the daily price return of AMD stock. It details AMDL's key statistics such as its $595.63 million AUM, 1.07% expense ratio, and its active management style without an underlying index. The analysis emphasizes that AMDL does not pay dividends and highlights its recent performance, including a significant yearly increase.

feed.title: Lenovo Vehicle Computing, WeRide Forge Partnership For Autonomous Driving Solutions
feed.summary: Lenovo Vehicle Computing and WeRide have partnered to develop Level 4 autonomous driving solutions for commercial applications. The collaboration will leverage Lenovo's AD1 domain controller, based on the NVIDIA DRIVE Thor platform, and integrate it into WeRide One, a versatile autonomous driving solution. This partnership aims to deliver enhanced autonomous driving products and services globally, focusing on safe and reliable commercial autonomous vehicles.

feed.title: AMDL Stock Price | GraniteShares 2x Long AMD Daily ETF
feed.summary: This article provides detailed information on the GraniteShares 2x Long AMD Daily ETF (AMDL), including its current stock price, trading exchange, and key financial metrics. It offers a general overview, holdings, historical data, and technical analysis, helping investors assess its performance and potential returns. The fund, managed by GraniteShares Advisors LLC, invests in equity markets, specifically in technology, semiconductors, and semiconductor equipment sectors using derivatives.

feed.title: Qualcomm Announces the Snapdragon 7+ Gen 3, Featuring Exceptional On-Device AI Capabilities
feed.summary: Qualcomm has unveiled the Snapdragon 7+ Gen 3 Mobile Platform, bringing on-device generative AI capabilities to the Snapdragon 7 series, including support for various large language models. The new platform also enhances gaming with Snapdragon Elite Gaming features like the Game Post Processing Accelerator and Adreno Frame Motion Engine 2, and introduces Wi-Fi 7 to the 7-series. Key OEMs such as OnePlus, realme, and SHARP are expected to adopt this platform in upcoming devices.

feed.title: AMZZ Stock Price | GraniteShares 2x Long AMZN Daily ETF
feed.summary: This article provides detailed information on the GraniteShares 2x Long AMZN Daily ETF (AMZZ), including its current stock price, trading exchange (NASDAQ), and key financial statistics like expense ratio and assets. It also offers insights into its performance, holdings, and technical analysis, helping investors assess its potential. The ETF aims to provide 2x leveraged daily returns designed to correspond to the performance of Amazon.com stock.

feed.title: What’s Inside Direxion’s Daily Semiconductor Bull and Bear 3x shares?
feed.summary: This article describes the Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull (SOXL) and Bear 3X Shares (SOXS) ETFs. It explains that these funds aim for daily investment results of 300%, or 300% of the inverse, of the performance of the NYSE Semiconductor Index, while cautioning that investment objectives are not guaranteed. The article also provides a link for more information about Direxion.

feed.title: Should You Invest in the Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ)?
feed.summary: The Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ) offers diversified and low-cost exposure to the Technology - Semiconductors sector, tracking the PHLX Semiconductor Sector Index. With $359.59 million in assets and a 0.19% expense ratio, it has shown strong performance, gaining 17.37% year-to-date and 57.63% in the last year as of March 26, 2024. The ETF holds a Zacks ETF Rank of 1 (Strong Buy), making it a recommended option for investors.

feed.title: SOSA aligned wideband multichannel RF digitizers from Analog Devices aim at reduced SWaP
feed.summary: Analog Devices has introduced the ADSY1100 series of wideband multichannel RF digitizers, designed in a 3U VPX SOSA-aligned format. These digitizers are intended for use in military and aerospace applications such as electronic warfare, radar, and communications, emphasizing reduced size, weight, and power (SWaP). The system features ADI's "Apollo" MXFE device with high DAC and ADC sample rates and integrated DSP to free up FPGA resources for mission-specific processing.

feed.title: AMD’s Lisa Su to speak at Computex
feed.summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su is scheduled to deliver the opening keynote speech at Computex Taipei on June 3, discussing the future of high-performance computing and AI. She will explore how AMD and its partners are advancing AI and HPC from data centers to end-user devices. The Computex event, themed "Connecting AI," will also feature other prominent tech leaders and is expected to include an AI cooperation announcement between Nvidia and Hon Hai.

feed.title: Newegg Makes Tech More Affordable for Students, Faculty and Staff with Affirm Offer
feed.summary: Newegg announced a new 10% discount for students, faculty, and staff when using an .edu email address and checking out with Affirm. This offer can be combined with other promotions, maximizing savings on technology products like laptops and PCs. The discount applies to orders over $100 and can be used multiple times, aiming to ease the financial burden of technology purchases for students.

feed.title: Aehr wins major order for SiC wafer test and burn-in system
feed.summary: Aehr Test Systems has secured a major order for its FOX-NP wafer level test and burn-in system for Silicon Carbide (SiC) devices from a prominent semiconductor company. This system, featuring new advanced voltage channel modules, will be used for engineering, qualification, and small lot production, with plans to transition to high-volume production using Aehr's FOX-XP systems. The customer, a leading semiconductor firm with global operations, is entering the SiC market to address applications in automotive, industrial, and electrification infrastructure.

feed.title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights Berkshire Hathaway, Procter & Gamble, Adobe, Advanced Micro Devices and AstraZeneca
feed.summary: This Zacks Analyst Blog highlights research reports on major stocks including Berkshire Hathaway, Procter & Gamble, and Adobe, along with Advanced Micro Devices and AstraZeneca PLC. It provides an overview of each company's recent performance, key financial drivers, and potential headwinds. The article emphasizes Zacks Investment Research's stock-picking strategies and their strong historical performance.

feed.title: Should You Invest in the Invesco AI and Next Gen Software ETF (IGPT)?
feed.summary: The Invesco AI and Next Gen Software ETF (IGPT) offers broad exposure to the Technology - Software segment, particularly in AI and next-gen software development. Launched in 2005, it is passively managed with an expense ratio of 0.60% and currently holds a Zacks ETF Rank of 1 (Strong Buy). Its top holdings include Meta Platforms, Nvidia, and Advanced Micro Devices, and it has shown strong year-to-date performance.

feed.title: The top performing funds that beat the S&P 500 in Q1
feed.summary: This article highlights the top-performing US equity funds in Q1 2024 that outperformed the S&P 500 index. Funds from GQG, Artemis, and Alger are featured, with GQG Partners U.S. Equity leading the pack. The piece details the investment strategies of these funds and specific holdings that contributed to their success.

feed.title: AMD Advances Open Source Initiative with MES Firmware Release for Radeon GPUs
feed.summary: AMD announced its plan to open-source the Micro Engine Scheduler (MES) firmware for its Radeon GPUs, enhancing user control and accessibility. This move directly addresses lobbying efforts from companies like Tiny Corp and could facilitate the integration of third-party chiplets. The company also anticipates significant growth from its MI300s, projecting substantial revenue increases in the coming fiscal years.

feed.title: TSMC Arizona and U.S. Department of Commerce Announce up to US$6.6 Billion in Proposed CHIPS Act Direct Funding, the Company Plans Third Leading-Edge Fab in Phoenix
feed.summary: TSMC Arizona and the U.S. Department of Commerce have signed a preliminary memorandum for up to US$6.6 billion in CHIPS Act direct funding. TSMC plans to build a third advanced semiconductor fab in Phoenix, Arizona, bringing its total investment in the site to over US$65 billion. This expansion aims to enhance U.S. semiconductor manufacturing capabilities and create thousands of high-tech jobs, with production from the three fabs utilizing 4nm, 2nm, and more advanced processes.

feed.title: TSMC's $6.6bn Arizona deal the latest in the CHIPS Act rollout
feed.summary: The Biden administration has announced a preliminary agreement for up to $6.6 billion in grants and $5 billion in loans for Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) under the CHIPS program. This investment, combined with TSMC's planned expansion in Arizona, totals $65 billion and aims to restart the semiconductor industry in the U.S. The Commerce Department expects the U.S. to produce about 20% of the world’s most advanced chips by 2030, a goal supported by the CHIPS funding and noted for its potential political implications in swing states.

feed.title: Acrivon To Privately Place $130 Mln Of Shares; Stock Soars In Pre-market
feed.summary: Acrivon Therapeutics, Inc. announced a private investment in public equity (PIPE) deal to sell 8,235,000 shares at $8.50 and pre-funded warrants, expecting to raise approximately $130 million. This funding will support the advancement of its lead clinical asset ACR-368, its preclinical pipeline, and the expansion of its AP3 platform and AI/machine learning capabilities. Following the announcement, Acrivon's stock (ACRV) soared by 72% in pre-market trading on the Nasdaq.

feed.title: AMD Extends Leadership Adaptive SoC Portfolio with New Versal Series Gen 2 Devices Delivering End-to-End Acceleration for AI-Driven Embedded Systems
feed.summary: AMD has announced the expansion of its Versal adaptive SoC portfolio with the new Versal AI Edge Series Gen 2 and Versal Prime Series Gen 2 devices, designed for end-to-end acceleration in AI-driven embedded systems. These devices offer significantly improved TOPs-per-watt and CPU-based scalar compute compared to the first generation, enabling single-chip intelligence for various applications. Subaru Corporation has already selected the Versal AI Edge Series Gen 2 for its next-generation EyeSight ADAS vision system.

feed.title: Intel challenges Nvidia with new Gaudi 3 AI chip as AMD expands processor lineup
feed.summary: Intel has launched its new Gaudi 3 AI chip, promising significantly improved performance over its predecessor and competitive speeds against Nvidia's H100 and H200 chips for training and inference of large language models. Simultaneously, AMD expanded its AI processor lineup with new Versal AI Edge Series Gen 2 and Prime Series Gen 2 chips, targeting edge computing devices and leveraging customizable FPGA technology. Both companies are pushing boundaries in the AI hardware space, with Intel also focusing on an open platform for enterprise AI and AMD enhancing its offerings for connected devices.

feed.title: BlackBerry and AMD to collaborate on new robotic systems tech
feed.summary: BlackBerry Ltd. has announced a collaboration with chipmaker AMD to develop new robotic systems technology. The partnership will combine BlackBerry's software with AMD's computer hardware, aiming to enhance sensor fusion, data processing, and real-time control in robotic applications. Following the announcement, BlackBerry's shares saw a significant rise on the Toronto Stock Exchange.

feed.title: BlackBerry gains on partnership with AMD for robotics systems
feed.summary: BlackBerry's shares surged over 7% following the announcement of a partnership with Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to develop a platform for advanced robotics in industrial and healthcare sectors. The collaboration, unveiled at the Embedded World conference, will leverage BlackBerry's ONX platform. Despite this gain, BlackBerry's U.S.-listed stock has seen a 13% decline year-to-date, contrasting with the S&P 500's 9% rise.

feed.title: 20 Chip Manufacturing Companies in India to Know
feed.summary: This article highlights 20 key chip manufacturing companies in India, emphasizing the country's growing importance in the semiconductor industry across design, fabrication, and materials development. It details how the rise of AI has heightened the demand for advanced chipsets, positioning India as a crucial hub for global semiconductor firms. The listed companies include major players like NVIDIA, AMD, Samsung, and Intel, alongside specialized Indian firms contributing to various aspects of semiconductor technology.

feed.title: Abbott Laboratories to close Bay Area site; almost 200 jobs to be cut
feed.summary: Abbott Laboratories is closing its Fairfield, California location later this year, leading to the layoff of 195 employees. The company, known for brands like Similac and Pedialyte, attributed a decrease in its annual sales to an anticipated decline in COVID-19 testing-related sales, despite overall sales growth in the fourth quarter. Fairfield Mayor Cathrine Moy expressed frustration over the timing of the layoffs and the company's decision to discontinue the nutritional bar previously made at the plant.

feed.title: Like Nvidia Stock but Prefer ETFs? This Is the Best Semiconductor ETF to Invest In Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Other Megatrends.
feed.summary: The VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) is highlighted as an excellent option for investors seeking exposure to the semiconductor industry and megatrends like AI, vehicle electrification, and autonomous driving, while mitigating the risk of investing in individual stocks like Nvidia (NVDA). The ETF has Nvidia as its largest holding, accounting for 20% of its portfolio, and has significantly outperformed the S&P 500 over various timeframes due to its focus on the robustly growing chip market. It provides diversification across the semiconductor value chain, including chip producers, foundries, and equipment manufacturers.

feed.title: AMD’s stock can surge 40%, this new bull says — even as Nvidia still dominates
feed.summary: HSBC analyst Frank Lee has upgraded AMD shares to buy from hold, setting a new price target of $225, which suggests a 40% upside. This comes despite Nvidia's dominance in the AI GPU market, as Lee believes there will be multiple winners in this sector. The upgrade reflects a positive outlook for AMD's performance in the burgeoning AI chip industry.

feed.title: AMD and Nvidia unveil new processors for AI PCs
feed.summary: AMD and Nvidia have both launched new processors designed for AI PCs, aiming to capitalize on the growing market for computers capable of running AI workloads on-device. AMD introduced its Ryzen PRO 8040 and 8000 Series processors, emphasizing their AI-enabled features for business users, while Nvidia unveiled new RTX A400 and A1000 GPUs with enhanced Tensor and RT Cores for accelerating AI and graphics tasks. This move by both companies highlights the intensifying competition in the AI PC sector, which analysts predict will drive new growth in the overall PC market.

feed.title: Avnet bullish on China's growth
feed.summary: Avnet, a US-based semiconductor distributor, is optimistic about China's long-term growth, citing opportunities in the digital economy and low-carbon transformation. The company plans to continue investing in the Chinese market, focusing on new energy facilities and supporting domestic chip manufacturers' global expansion. This confidence is bolstered by China's emphasis on technological innovation and the resilience of its semiconductor industry despite challenges.

feed.title: Intel Builds World’s Largest Neuromorphic System to Enable More Sustainable AI
feed.summary: Intel has developed Hala Point, the world's largest neuromorphic system, featuring 1.15 billion neurons and 1,152 Loihi 2 processors to address the efficiency and sustainability challenges of AI. Deployed at Sandia National Laboratories, this system aims to advance brain-inspired AI research and future real-time continuous learning for various applications. It achieves state-of-the-art computational efficiencies, rivaling and exceeding GPU and CPU architectures, and promises significant energy savings for future AI deployments.

feed.title: Cadence Supercharges Chip Debug And Prototyping With Palladium Z3 And Protium X3 Systems
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems has launched its new Palladium Z3 and Protium X3 systems, designed to significantly enhance chip debugging and advanced prototyping. The Palladium Z3 offers 1.5 times the performance and double the capacity of its predecessor for pre-silicon debugging, while the Protium X3, built with AMD Versal Premium VP1902 adaptive SoCs, provides 1.5 times faster performance and triple the capacity for advanced physical prototyping. These systems are crucial for accelerating the development of complex chips in the AI era, with general availability expected in Q3 2024.

feed.title: Steering Clients Through Unstable Markets
feed.summary: Paul Schatz, president of Heritage Capital, discussed with etf.com his strategies for navigating client portfolios through current market instability characterized by higher inflation and interest rates. He has reduced exposure to "hot" AI and tech stocks, finding new opportunities in "unloved" sectors like pharmaceuticals, chemicals, and staples. Schatz also shared his market outlook, anticipating a first-quarter peak followed by a minor pullback and potential for fresh highs over the summer, while also discussing his cautious approach to bonds.

feed.title: Micron Poised to Get Over $6 Billion in Chips Grants in Announcement Next Week
feed.summary: Micron Technology Inc. is expected to receive $6.1 billion in grants from the Commerce Department under the CHIPS Act. This funding will support domestic factory projects as part of efforts to boost US semiconductor production. The announcement is anticipated next week, and Micron, like Intel and TSMC, will also accept loans as part of its award package.

feed.title: 4 Undervalued Semiconductor Stocks
feed.summary: Amidst the AI boom led by Nvidia, Morningstar analysts identify Infineon Technologies, STMicroelectronics, MediaTek, and Skyworks Solutions as undervalued semiconductor stocks. The article discusses the varying valuations between digital and analog chipmakers, highlighting the robust demand for AI-related semiconductors versus the mixed recovery in non-AI sectors like automotive, industrial, and personal electronics. It provides detailed reasons for each company's valuation and market position.

feed.title: US to Provide $6.1 Billion for Micron Technology Chip Plants
feed.summary: The Biden administration will provide $6.1 billion in government support for Micron Technology to produce advanced memory computer chips in New York and Idaho. This funding, from the 2022 CHIPS and Science Act, aims to revive the manufacturing sector, create 50,000 jobs, and bolster domestic semiconductor production. Micron plans to invest a total of $100 billion in upstate New York over two decades and $15 billion in Boise, Idaho.

feed.title: The Latest Breakthroughs in Quantum Computing
feed.summary: This article discusses recent breakthroughs in quantum computing, focusing on two key developments. Researchers at Kyushu University have created a qubit that achieved quantum coherence at room temperature for 100 nanoseconds, a significant step towards practical quantum computers. Additionally, scientists have found that thin films of bismuth display the non-linear Hall effect at room temperature, which could lead to new components for high-speed electronics and prevent supercomputers from overheating.

feed.title: TSMC Accelerates Nearshoring to Diversify Away from China
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) is significantly expanding its manufacturing presence in the United States, increasing its investment from $40 billion to $65 billion, primarily in Phoenix, Arizona. This strategic move, supported by $6.6 billion in U.S. government subsidies, aims to decentralize chip production away from Taiwan, enhance U.S. technological sovereignty, and create thousands of jobs. The expansion reflects a broader industry trend towards nearshoring and diversifying supply chains to mitigate geopolitical risks and reduce reliance on Asian markets for advanced semiconductor manufacturing.

feed.title: A Brief and Personal History of EDA, Part 5: The Acquisition Era
feed.summary: This article, part five of a personal history of EDA, details the acquisition era of Electronic Design Automation (EDA) and the significant role played by leading companies like Cadence, Synopsys, and Mentor Graphics. It highlights the aggressive acquisition strategies employed by these firms to dominate the market and lists numerous acquired companies and their technologies, emphasizing the dynamic nature of the EDA industry. The author also touches on the role of consultants like Jim Hogan in fostering and preparing startups for acquisition.

feed.title: Cadence emulator and prototyping duo can handle up to 48 billion gate SoCs
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems has introduced its third-generation Palladium Z3 emulation and Protium X3 FPGA prototyping systems, offering significant capacity and performance improvements for advanced SoC development. These systems can handle designs from 16 million up to 48 billion gates, enabling comprehensive testing of large SoCs. Powered by Nvidia's BlueField DPU and Quantum InfiniBand, the solutions are designed to accelerate hardware verification and software validation, especially for AI, automotive, and hyperscale applications.

feed.title: The Top 3 Gaming Stocks to Buy in April 2024
feed.summary: This article highlights three top gaming stocks to consider buying in April 2024: Take-Two Interactive (TTWO), Electronic Arts (EA), and the VanEck Vectors Video Gaming and eSports ETF (ESPO). It notes the explosive growth in video game sales, exceeding $57 billion in 2023, and anticipates further growth with upcoming game releases like Grand Theft Auto VI. The piece details reasons to invest in each, including oversold positions, strong earnings, and diversification benefits.

feed.title: Advanced Packaging Market Aims for Remarkable Growth, Targeting USD 79.9 Billion by 2033 at 8.4% CAGR
feed.summary: The advanced packaging market is projected to grow from USD 35.7 billion in 2023 to USD 79.9 billion by 2033, achieving an 8.4% CAGR. This growth is driven by the increasing demand for miniaturization of devices and advanced packaging solutions for integrated circuits in various sectors, especially consumer electronics and automotive. The Asia Pacific region is anticipated to dominate the market due to its prominent semiconductor manufacturing industry.

feed.title: Micron Becomes First Brand to Produce 200+ Layer QLC NAND SSDs for Client and Data Centers
feed.summary: Micron Technology announced that its 232-layer QLC NAND is now in mass production and shipping in select Crucial SSDs, as well as being sampled to OEM PC manufacturers in the Micron 2500 NVMe SSD. This makes Micron the first brand to produce 200+ layer QLC NAND SSDs for both client and data center applications, offering significant improvements in bit-density, I/O speeds, and read/programming performance. The Micron 2500 client SSD is highlighted for its ability to enhance the PC user experience and accelerate QLC adoption with its superior performance and endurance.

feed.title: Statement by Acting Secretary Su on announcement of construction of new semiconductor fabrication sites in New York, Idaho
feed.summary: Acting Secretary Julie Su issued a statement announcing Micron Technology Inc.'s plans to build three new semiconductor fabrication facilities in New York and Idaho, expected to create over 70,000 jobs. The announcement highlights the Biden-Harris administration's "made in America" economic strategy through the CHIPS and Science Act, emphasizing Micron's commitment to pro-worker practices, including Project Labor Agreements, respecting workers' right to organize, providing childcare, and expanding Registered Apprenticeship programs. Micron is also joining the Department of Labor’s Advanced Manufacturing Sector Table to ensure a diverse and job-ready manufacturing workforce.

feed.title: UMC reports lowest profit in three years
feed.summary: United Microelectronic Corp (UMC) reported its lowest net profit in nearly three years in the first quarter, at NT$10.46 billion, due to prolonged inventory correction. However, the company anticipates a 3 percent increase in wafer shipments for the current quarter, driven by improving demand in communication and computer devices. UMC maintains a cautiously optimistic outlook for the year, expecting to outperform the global foundry industry and forecasting global semiconductor industry growth of about 5 percent, largely led by AI servers.

feed.title: CEOs of Microsoft, Nvidia and other tech giants join federal AI advisory board
feed.summary: A federal AI advisory board has been established, comprising prominent tech executives, academics, and civil rights leaders, including CEOs from Microsoft, Nvidia, and OpenAI. Led by Secretary of Homeland Security Alejandro Mayorkas, the board will advise the Department of Homeland Security on the safe application of AI in critical infrastructure and issue recommendations for organizations like power grid operators and manufacturers. This initiative stems from President Biden's executive order on AI, which also mandates data sharing on new AI models' safety with the government.

feed.title: AMD Q1 Earnings Preview: Analysts Bullish, But Warn Of Factors That 'Can Derail The Semiconductor Industry'
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) is set to release its Q1 earnings, with analysts expecting revenue of $5.46 billion and earnings of 61 cents per share. While many analysts are bullish due to strong AI MI300 and EPYC performance, they also highlight risks such as market share declines, intense competition, and macroeconomic uncertainties that could impact the semiconductor industry. AMD shares have performed well over the last year, but some analysts have adjusted price targets ahead of the report.

feed.title: AMD Earnings: A snapshot of Advanced Micro Devices’ Q1 2024 results
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) reported an increase in revenues and adjusted earnings for the first quarter of 2024. Adjusted earnings rose to $0.62 per share, and March-quarter revenues increased to $5.47 billion, signaling a recovery in demand. The company also shifted from a net loss in the prior-year period to a net income of $123 million in Q1 2024.

feed.title: 4 Undervalued Semiconductor Stocks
feed.summary: Despite the AI boom driving up semiconductor stock prices, Morningstar analysts have identified four undervalued semiconductor stocks: Infineon Technologies, STMicroelectronics, MediaTek, and Skyworks Solutions. While digital chipmakers are largely overvalued due to AI, analog/mixed-signal chip valuations are still below fair value estimates. These top picks are favored for their strong positions in automotive semis, leadership in silicon-carbide, expansion into new markets, and essential RF products for mobile devices.

feed.title: Nvidia Could Help This Stock-Split ETF Turn $200,000 Into $1 Million in 10 Years
feed.summary: The iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX), which holds significant stakes in leading chip companies like Nvidia, recently completed a 3-for-1 stock split, making it more accessible to investors. Driven by the booming artificial intelligence sector and the need to upgrade data center infrastructure, the ETF has shown impressive historical returns. With continued growth in AI, the SOXX ETF has the potential to turn a $200,000 investment into $1 million within a decade, even with moderate annual returns.

feed.title: Jaguar Health, Inc. (JAGX) Surges 30.31% Amid High Volume: Technical and Fundamental Analysis
feed.summary: Jaguar Health, Inc. (JAGX) saw its stock surge by 30.31% to $0.29 with high trading volume, indicating strong investor interest but also overbought conditions with an RSI of 85. Despite bullish technicals showing the price above moving averages, fundamental analysis reveals significant losses with negative EPS and PE ratio. The article concludes with a "Hold" recommendation due to high volatility, speculative nature, and fundamental weaknesses, advising investors to monitor for improvement in financials, especially after the upcoming earnings announcement.

feed.title: AMD is the latest company to show that AI is an expensive proposition
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) shares slid 9% after its latest earnings report, highlighting that artificial intelligence is an expensive endeavor for both chip buyers and manufacturers. AMD discussed increased investment spending to support its AI product portfolio, which is impacting the gross-margin profile for its data-center graphics processing units. This indicates that while AI presents significant opportunities, it also demands substantial financial commitment.

feed.title: AMD AI chip revenue forecast fails to impress, shares dive 7%
authors: Arsheeya Bajwa and Max A. Cherney
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares fell by about 7% after its forecast for AI chip sales for 2024, at roughly $4 billion, failed to meet Wall Street's expectations, despite being an increase from its prior estimate. The company faces stiff competition from Nvidia in the booming AI server semiconductor market and is experiencing weakness in traditional server, gaming, and embedded segments. AMD also reported first-quarter revenue that slightly beat estimates and provided a second-quarter revenue forecast in line with analyst expectations.

feed.title: AMD, Super Micro tumble as earnings fall short
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Super Micro Computer experienced significant stock drops after their earnings forecasts and results fell short of Wall Street's high expectations for AI chip sales and revenue. Despite the downturn, some analysts remain positive about AMD's future as easing supply chain constraints could boost its AI chip market share. The selloff affected other AI-linked chip companies, contributing to a 3.5% tumble in the Philadelphia Semiconductor index.

feed.title: Astera Labs Inc call volume above normal and directionally bullish
feed.summary: Astera Labs Inc. (ALAB) is experiencing unusually high and bullish call option volume, with 2,217 calls trading, exceeding expected levels, and implied volatility increasing. The most active options are the May-24 80 calls and Jul-24 100 calls, accounting for nearly 920 contracts, while the Put/Call Ratio stands at 0.33. Earnings for Astera Labs are anticipated on May 7th.

feed.title: AI In Chip Design Market Size, Share | CAGR of 31.4%
feed.summary: The global AI in chip design market is projected to reach USD 27.6 billion by 2033, growing from USD 1.8 billion in 2023 at a CAGR of 31.4%. This growth is driven by the increasing integration of AI in consumer electronics and the expansion of AI applications in sectors like healthcare, despite challenges such as high development costs and technological complexity. North America currently dominates the market due to its robust technological infrastructure and significant investments in R&D.

feed.title: Who’s making chips for AI? Chinese manufacturers lag behind US tech giants
feed.summary: Chinese researchers are reportedly 5-10 years behind their US counterparts in AI chip manufacturing due to US export restrictions. The rapid advancements in generative AI have intensified a global race for computer chips, highlighting China's struggle to keep pace with key technologies under these limitations.

feed.title: Rapid7 Earnings: What To Look For From RPD
feed.summary: Cybersecurity software maker Rapid7 (NASDAQ: RPD) is poised to release its earnings report tomorrow after market close. Analysts are expecting revenue growth of 11.4% year-on-year to $204 million and adjusted earnings of $0.54 per share. The company's stock has seen a 7.1% decline in the last month amidst broader volatility in growth stocks and an average analyst price target of $59.7.

feed.title: ST rolls a new FD-SOI microcontroller with phase-change memory (PCM) for automotive applications
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics has announced a new generation of automotive microcontrollers featuring embedded phase-change memory (ePCM), fabricated by Samsung using a jointly developed 18nm FD-SOI process. These microcontrollers target the automotive industry with its stringent temperature requirements, addressing a historical challenge for PCM technology. ST plans to sample these devices to automotive customers in the second half of 2024, with production slated for the latter half of 2025.

feed.title: Robert Dennard, Inventor of Computer DRAM at IBM, Dies at 91
feed.summary: Robert Dennard, the IBM engineer credited with inventing dynamic random access memory (DRAM) in the late 1960s, has passed away at the age of 91. His groundbreaking invention was crucial in the development of personal computers, mobile phones, tablets, and video game players. Dennard's death was announced on April 23, according to a notice in the Journal News.

feed.title: Should You Buy Nvidia Stock Before the AI Chipmaker Reports Earnings on May 22?
feed.summary: Nvidia's stock has seen a significant surge due to demand for its GPUs driven by generative AI. With first-quarter earnings approaching on May 22nd, analysts have high expectations for the chipmaker. Investors are advised to be cautious due to the stock's current pricey valuation, despite its strong market position and growth potential in the AI economy.

feed.title: Jaguar Health (JAGX) Surges 15.74%: Technical & Fundamental Analysis, Stock Outlook & Long-term Potential
feed.summary: Jaguar Health (JAGX) saw a 15.74% surge in its stock price, closing at $0.3095, driven by strong buying momentum. Technical indicators show overbought conditions and a bullish trend, with key resistance and support levels identified. The company's upcoming Q1 2024 financials and corporate updates webcast on May 14, 2024, are anticipated to significantly impact market sentiment and dictate short-term fluctuations, although long-term potential remains uncertain due to ongoing profitability challenges.

feed.title: Nvidia's next-generation GPU revealed: integrating eight HBM 4, TSMC N3 process
feed.summary: Nvidia's upcoming R100 AI chip is expected to enter mass production in Q4 2025, utilizing TSMC's N3 process and integrating eight HBM4s. The company is also focusing on improving energy consumption alongside AI computing power in its next-generation chips. This aggressive roadmap reflects Nvidia's strategy to accelerate product release cycles to maintain market leadership against competitors like AMD.

feed.title: Liqid composability tech aims to turn Dell servers into GPU powerhouses
feed.summary: Liqid's composability technology aims to transform Dell servers into powerful AI model training systems by enabling a single Dell R760 server to access up to 30 NVIDIA L40S GPUs. This UltraStack configuration, developed with Dell and NVIDIA, promises significant reductions in capital and operational expenses while boosting AI performance, efficiency, and sustainability. Liqid's SmartStack technology also allows efficient allocation of GPU resources to multiple clients or modular server nodes, supporting various Dell PowerEdge systems.

feed.title: HP Unlocks New Era of AI Experiences Powering Work and Creation
feed.summary: HP has unveiled new AI PCs, the HP OmniBook X AI PC and HP EliteBook Ultra AI PC, designed with ARM architecture and Snapdragon X Elite processors to power AI experiences locally. These devices aim to enhance productivity and creativity with features like Copilot+ PC and improved collaboration tools. HP highlights their sleek design, long battery life, sustainability, and robust security for both consumer and commercial users.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices to set up an R&D center in Taiwan, ministry says
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has applied to establish a research and development (R&D) center in Taiwan under the government's "A+ Industrial Innovative R&D Program." The investment is estimated at NT$5 billion (US$155 million), with the ministry setting conditions such as collaborating with Taiwanese IC designers and recruiting foreign talent. This move is part of Taiwan's broader strategy to become a global R&D hub, attracting major tech companies like ASML, Lam Research, Applied Materials, and Nvidia.

feed.title: AMD Instinct MI300X Accelerators Power Microsoft Azure OpenAI Service Workloads and New Azure ND MI300X V5 VMs
feed.summary: AMD announced that its Instinct MI300X accelerators are powering Microsoft Azure OpenAI Service workloads and the new Azure ND MI300X V5 VMs. These VMs are now generally available, with Hugging Face as a key customer, leveraging the MI300X and ROCm software to achieve leading price/performance for GPT workloads. The collaboration also extends to AMD Ryzen AI processors for edge deployments and Alveo MA35D media accelerators for live streaming video services, highlighting a broad partnership between AMD and Microsoft in AI and enterprise computing.

feed.title: Supermicro Introduces High-Density, Efficient, and Cost Optimized Solutions Powered by the AMD EPYC™ 4004 Series Processors
feed.summary: Supermicro has announced new additions to its H13 generation of CPU Servers, featuring AMD EPYC 4004 Series processors, designed for high-density, efficiency, and cost optimization. These solutions include a 3U MicroCloud multi-node server capable of supporting up to ten nodes for cloud-native workloads, offering significant density improvements and reduced TCO for data centers. The new servers are targeted at cloud hosting providers, SMBs, and departmental customers, providing powerful yet compact computing for various applications.

feed.title: Opinion: Intel and AMD take a backseat as Qualcomm plays starring role in Microsoft’s AI PCs
feed.summary: Microsoft unveiled a new line of AI-enabled PCs, branded as Copilot + PCs, which are powered by Qualcomm's Snapdragon processors instead of Intel's. This move positions Qualcomm as a key player in Microsoft's AI PC strategy, potentially shifting market dynamics for traditional chip partners Intel and AMD. The article highlights Qualcomm's significant headway in the AI PC arena.

feed.title: NVIDIA Announces Financial Results for First Quarter Fiscal 2025
feed.summary: NVIDIA reported record quarterly revenue of $26.0 billion for Q1 FY25, a 262% increase year-over-year, driven by strong demand for AI in its Data Center segment. The company also announced a ten-for-one forward stock split and a 150% increase in its quarterly cash dividend. NVIDIA outlined significant platform advancements, including the Blackwell platform and Spectrum-X, positioning itself for continued growth in the AI market.

feed.title: Trading Strategies For Nvidia Stock Before And After Q1 Earnings, A Top Holding In Spear Invest ETF (SPRX)
feed.summary: This article discusses trading strategies for Nvidia stock ahead of its Q1 earnings report, noting its historical post-earnings surges and current technical indicators. It also highlights the Spear Alpha ETF (SPRX) as a diversified investment option for those bullish on Nvidia, given Nvidia's significant weighting in the fund alongside other tech companies like AMD and Snowflake. The ETF focuses on long-term capital growth by investing in companies capitalizing on emerging trends in industrial technology.

feed.title: With the rise of Ethernet, Nvidia Infiniband is being encroached upon
feed.summary: Nvidia's InfiniBand sales are being encroached upon by the growing demand for Ethernet in data centers, particularly from hyperscale enterprises and cloud builders. While InfiniBand still accounts for a large portion of Nvidia's network sales, the company is actively pushing its Spectrum X Ethernet products, expecting them to become a multi-billion dollar product line. Despite this internal cannibalization, Nvidia anticipates continued strong growth, with CEO Jensen Huang emphasizing the company's commitment to both InfiniBand and Ethernet as computing structures, alongside NVLink.

feed.title: SOXS ETF: iflas etmenin başka bir pahalı yolu
feed.summary: The Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bear 3x Shares (SOXS) ETF is highlighted as a high-cost, underperforming investment, primarily due to its inverse relationship with the thriving semiconductor industry. Despite a massive 1.03% expense ratio, the ETF has shown significant negative returns, losing over 99.96% in the last five years, while its counterpart, SOXL, has seen substantial gains. This poor performance is attributed to the sustained growth of the semiconductor sector, fueled by demand in areas like AI and data centers, as evidenced by strong earnings from companies like Nvidia.

feed.title: AMD, Workday, Alibaba, Super Micro Computer, and Other Tech Stocks in Focus Today
feed.summary: Tech stocks, including AMD, Workday, Alibaba, and Super Micro Computer, are in focus today. The rebound comes after Nvidia's strong earnings report failed to prevent losses in the previous trading session. The article highlights the current market sentiment surrounding these technology companies.

feed.title: Data Center Powerplay: The Chips Have to Go Somewhere
feed.summary: Citi Research forecasts a significant surge in data center capacity, driven by the power demands of Generative AI (GenAI), expecting a compound annual growth of 17% to 100 GW by 2030, up from 33 GW in 2023. GenAI workloads are projected to account for over half of this demand by 2030, with concerns raised about utilities' ability to meet the escalating power needs. The report estimates annual peak power requirements for US data centers to be 5 GW to 8 GW through 2030, posing a potential bottleneck if supply constraints persist.

feed.title: Astera Labs Extends Interoperability Leadership Driving PCIe 6.x Deployment
feed.summary: Astera Labs has expanded the PCIe 6.x testing capabilities of its Cloud-Scale Interop Lab to ensure interoperability between its Aries 6 PCIe/CXL Smart DSP Retimers and various PCIe 6.x hosts and endpoints. This initiative aims to accelerate the deployment of high-bandwidth, low-latency PCIe 6.x connectivity in AI platforms, reducing development time. The company also launched its first Cloud-Scale Interop Lab outside Silicon Valley in Taiwan to foster closer collaboration with ODM customers.

feed.title: HP CEO: AI PCs To Make Up A Tenth Of Shipments In Fiscal Year’s Second Half
feed.summary: HP CEO Enrique Lores predicts that AI PCs will constitute 10% of the company's client computer shipments in the latter half of its fiscal year, driven initially by consumer sales. He anticipates a significant increase in AI PC demand over the next few years, forecasting that AI PCs will represent 40-50% of total PC shipments within three years. Despite a decline in printing revenue, HP's Personal Systems business saw growth in commercial PC sales.

feed.title: SoFi, AMD, Robinhood, Salesforce, and Other Tech Stocks in Focus Today
feed.summary: Tech stocks, including SoFi, AMD, Robinhood, and Salesforce, experienced a downturn early on Wednesday, signalling a loss of momentum in their recent rally. This dip is attributed to broader market weakness impacting the sector. The article highlights these specific companies as being in focus due to the market's response.

feed.title: Samsung partner Corning enters glass substrate market as AI chips boom
feed.summary: Corning, a specialty glass producer and long-time Samsung partner, is entering the glass substrate market for semiconductors due to increasing demand for AI chips. The company is sampling glass substrates to global customers, including those in Korea, and anticipates this market will surpass its existing chip business. Corning plans to invest 2 trillion Korean won ($1.5 billion) in Korea by 2028 to support bendable glass production and facility upgrades, with its R&D center in Asan playing a key role.

feed.title: Stocks to Watch | Nvidia Stock: 4 Reasons to Buy, 4 Reasons to Sell
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of Nvidia's stock, outlining four reasons to buy and four reasons to sell. It discusses Nvidia's impressive Q1 fiscal 2025 earnings, its dominance in the AI market, and its valuation compared to competitors, while also addressing concerns about competition, regulatory challenges, and potential supply gluts. Ultimately, the author concludes that Nvidia's strengths still outweigh its weaknesses.

feed.title: Marvell Expands Connectivity Portfolio With New PCIe Retimer Product Line to Scale Compute Fabrics of Accelerated Infrastructure
feed.summary: Marvell Technology Inc. has launched its new Alaska P PCIe retimer product line, designed to scale data center compute fabrics in accelerated servers, CXL systems, and disaggregated infrastructure. These 8- and 16-lane PCIe Gen 6 retimers, built on Marvell's 5nm PAM4 technology, are crucial for connecting AI accelerators, GPUs, and CPUs as AI applications demand significantly higher bandwidth and faster data rates, necessitating reliable communication over greater distances. The new product line extends Marvell's PAM4 leadership beyond Ethernet and InfiniBand, addressing the growing market for PCIe and CXL connectivity within server systems with industry-leading low power consumption.

feed.title: More Than Half Of HDDs Could Use HAMR By 2027
feed.summary: Toshiba announced it will ship HAMR HDDs over 30TB in 2025, following Seagate's anticipated volume production in late 2024. The article predicts that by 2026-2027, all three major HDD manufacturers will be producing HAMR drives in volume, potentially seeing over half of all shipped HDDs utilize HAMR technology to maintain a lower cost per TB compared to SSDs for nearline storage. Western Digital's strategy for HAMR adoption is also discussed, suggesting they might follow rapidly due to shared component suppliers.

feed.title: Computer Industry Joins NVIDIA to Build AI Factories and Data Centers for the Next Industrial Revolution
feed.summary: NVIDIA, in collaboration with top computer manufacturers, unveiled a new array of NVIDIA Blackwell architecture-powered systems, featuring Grace CPUs and NVIDIA networking, designed for building AI factories and data centers. These systems aim to drive the next wave of generative AI breakthroughs, with offerings ranging from single to multi-GPUs and various cooling technologies. Key industry players like ASRock Rack, ASUS, GIGABYTE, and Supermicro are among those delivering these advanced AI systems.

feed.title: ‘Accelerate Everything,’ NVIDIA CEO Says Ahead of COMPUTEX
feed.summary: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang, speaking ahead of COMPUTEX, emphasized a "major shift in computing" driven by AI and accelerated computing, detailing new semiconductors, software, and systems. Huang announced a one-year rhythm for new semiconductors, including the upcoming Rubin platform, and highlighted accelerated computing as sustainable, driving down costs. He also showcased strong industry adoption of NVIDIA technologies for AI factories, next-generation networking with Spectrum-X, and the transformation of generative AI development with NVIDIA NIM, alongside the integration of AI assistants into RTX AI PCs and the adoption of NVIDIA Robotics for industrial digitization.

feed.title: AMD Extends AI and High-Performance Leadership in Data Center and PCs with New AMD Instinct, Ryzen and EPYC Processors at Computex 2024
feed.summary: At Computex 2024, AMD unveiled an expanded roadmap for its Instinct accelerators, including the MI325X arriving in Q4 2024 and the MI350 series in 2025, demonstrating a commitment to annual leadership AI accelerator releases. They also introduced new Ryzen AI 300 Series processors for Copilot+ PCs and Ryzen 9000 Series desktop processors, emphasizing AI and high-performance computing across data centers and consumer devices. Additionally, AMD previewed 5th Gen EPYC server processors for 2H 2024 and showcased leadership in edge AI innovation with Versal AI Edge Series Gen 2, highlighting partnerships with Microsoft, HP, Lenovo, and Asus.

feed.title: NVIDIA Brings AI Assistants to Life With GeForce RTX AI PCs
feed.summary: NVIDIA announced new RTX technology to power AI assistants and digital humans on new GeForce RTX AI laptops, including "Project G-Assist" for context-aware help in games and apps. They also introduced PC-based NVIDIA NIM inference microservices for the NVIDIA ACE digital human platform, enabled by the new NVIDIA RTX AI Toolkit. These innovations aim to accelerate over 500 PC applications and games, with new RTX AI PC laptops from ASUS and MSI featuring up to GeForce RTX 4070 GPUs.

feed.title: AMD-Nvidia ETFs in Focus Amid New Chip Introductions
feed.summary: AMD and Nvidia are at the forefront of AI chip development, with both companies introducing new generations of AI processors to meet surging demand. AMD announced its MI325X accelerator and future MI350 and MI400 series, while Nvidia unveiled its Rubin platform, committing to annual chip releases. The intense competition and rapid advancements in AI semiconductor technology are drawing significant investor interest, with several ETFs offering exposure to both chipmakers.

feed.title: AMD announces new AI chips amid intensifying competition with Nvidia, Intel
feed.summary: AMD announced new AI chips at the Computex tech conference in Taipei, emphasizing AI as its top priority and a transformative force in the computing market. This move intensifies its competition with rivals Nvidia and Intel, both of whom are also rapidly introducing new AI-focused processors. AMD plans to release new AI chip technology annually, outlining a roadmap for Instinct accelerators and EPYC server processors through 2026.

feed.title: AMD debuts ‘world’s fastest’ consumer desktop processor and AI-optimized server chips
feed.summary: AMD has unveiled its Ryzen 9 9950X, a new consumer desktop CPU that the company claims is the fastest in its category. Additionally, AMD introduced two AI-optimized laptop chips, the Ryzen AI 300 Series, and detailed three upcoming machine learning accelerators aimed at the data center AI market, including the Instinct MI325X, MI350, and MI400. The company also announced its Epyc Turin server CPU line, based on the new Zen 5 architecture, which promises significant AI performance upgrades.

feed.title: Coherent Appoints Jim Anderson as CEO
feed.summary: Coherent Corp. (NYSE: COHR) has appointed Jim Anderson as its new Chief Executive Officer, effective June 3, 2024. Anderson, who previously served as President and CEO of Lattice Semiconductors, also joins Coherent's Board of Directors. He succeeds Dr. Vincent D. Mattera, Jr., who is retiring, and is expected to drive innovation and transformation at Coherent, leveraging his experience to capitalize on strong market demand in areas like AI-related datacom.

feed.title: Coherent Corp. names Lattice Semiconductor executive as CEO; shares rise in early trading
feed.summary: Coherent Corp. has appointed Jim Anderson, former CEO of Lattice Semiconductor, as its new chief executive, succeeding Vincent "Chuck" Mattera Jr. Following the announcement, Coherent shares experienced a 16% increase in early trading. Anderson brings extensive semiconductor industry experience from companies like Intel and Advanced Micro Devices, and his compensation package includes $48 million in equity awards.

feed.title: Lattice Semiconductor Plunges as CEO Departs For Coherent, Whose Stock Soars
feed.summary: Shares of Lattice Semiconductor plunged nearly 16% after CEO Jim Anderson announced his departure to lead Coherent Corp. In contrast, Coherent's stock jumped 23% on the news. Lattice has appointed Chief Marketing Officer Esam Elashmawi as interim CEO while they search for a permanent replacement.

feed.title: Cisco Live Day 1 news brings together networking and security
feed.summary: Cisco Live's 2024 event highlights the company's focus on integrating networking and security, especially in the context of AI's growing influence. Cisco announced innovations like Nexus HyperFabric with Nvidia and Nutanix, and advancements in its Security Cloud, aiming to simplify operations and enhance threat protection across its installed base. The company emphasizes a strategy of "melting security into the network" to leverage its heritage and address the evolving tech landscape.

feed.title: AMD, TSMC to produce new chips
feed.summary: AMD will collaborate with TSMC to produce its next-generation chips using 3-nanometer process technology, despite speculation about using Samsung's technology. AMD CEO Lisa Su emphasized the strong partnership with TSMC and Taiwan's importance to the semiconductor ecosystem. She also discussed an annual update cycle for AMD's AI chips, with the Instinct MI325X planned for release later this year.

feed.title: Which AI Stocks Are Turning Hype Into Revenue?
feed.summary: This article identifies AI stocks that have successfully converted hype into tangible revenue and growth during the first fiscal quarter. It highlights how companies like Nvidia, Microsoft, Amazon, Arista Networks, Broadcom, and Marvell Technology have seen increased fair value estimates due to strong demand for their AI-related products and services. While many AI-related valuations are considered high, the author suggests investment opportunities in Nvidia, certain auto industry stocks, and undervalued software and cybersecurity companies such as Adobe, Fortinet, and Palo Alto Networks.

feed.title: ASML to Ship Its $380 Million Chip Machine to TSMC This Year
feed.summary: ASML Holding NV is set to ship its latest $380 million high-NA extreme ultraviolet (EUV) chipmaking machine to Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) and Intel Corp. by the end of this year. This latest technology is crucial for advanced chip manufacturing. TSMC had previously expressed concerns about the machine's cost, while Intel has already received its first unit.

feed.title: Nvidia’s Huang champions Supermicro’s direct liquid cooling tech
feed.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang publicly endorsed Supermicro's direct liquid cooling (DLC) technology for AI data centers at Computex Taipei, alongside Supermicro CEO Charles Liang. Huang highlighted DLC as crucial for handling the exponential growth in data processing driven by generative AI, estimating a US$3 trillion investment in new data centers by 2030 will boost demand for Supermicro's solutions. Liang emphasized DLC's energy and cost-saving benefits over traditional air cooling, aiming for it to become a mainstream solution for AI data centers efficiency.

feed.title: TSMC Shares Hit Record High as AI Demand Boosts Chip Industry
feed.summary: Shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC), the world's largest contract chip maker, reached a new record high due to surging global demand for advanced AI chips. The company's stock rose 5.15% in Taipei, pushing its market capitalization to $717.58 billion and contributing to an all-time high for the benchmark Taiex Index. This surge also follows recent announcements of a new share buyback program and a strategic partnership.

feed.title: Coherent Corp. Gets a New CEO
feed.summary: Coherent Corp. announced James R. Anderson as its new CEO, replacing Vincent D. Mattera, Jr. Anderson's appointment, following his successful tenure at Lattice Semiconductor, caused Coherent's stock to jump 23% while Lattice's fell 15.5%. The move signals Coherent's focus on AI, datacom, and industrial markets, building on Mattera's legacy of significant growth and acquisitions.

feed.title: NVIDIA and AMD Make Their Next Move in the AI Chip War
feed.summary: NVIDIA and AMD recently unveiled their next-generation AI chips, with NVIDIA introducing its "Rubin" architecture and AMD launching its Ryzen AI 300 and Ryzen 9000 series. While both companies are making significant advancements in the AI chip market, the author argues that NVIDIA remains the stronger investment due to its superior fundamentals and market performance, despite AMD's efforts to increase its market share in AI-powered PCs. The article advises investors to look for fundamentally strong companies rather than falling for general AI hype.

feed.title: Microcontroller operations in an OCP ORV3 smart battery backup unit
feed.summary: This article details the main microcontroller's software operations in an Open Compute Project (OCP) Open Rack Version 3 (ORV3) smart battery backup unit (BBU), following up on a previous discussion of its electrical and mechanical design. It outlines six critical processes managed by the microcontroller, including housekeeping, charging, discharging, fault handling, and Modbus communication, ensuring the BBU's high efficiency and capacity in the new 48V architecture. The piece emphasizes the collaboration between hardware and software to deliver reliable system-level solutions.

feed.title: Who are the highest-paid S&P 500 CEOs of 2023?
feed.summary: The median total compensation for S&P 500 CEOs increased by 12.6% in 2023, reaching $16.3 million, largely due to stock awards. Broadcom Inc CEO Hock Tan was the highest-paid chief executive with over $161 million, significantly more than other top earners like Fair Isaac Corporation CEO William Lansing and Apple CEO Tim Cook. This surge in CEO pay outpaced the 5.2% increase for the median S&P 500 employee, further widening the CEO-to-employee pay ratio.

feed.title: Nvidia's Stock Split Could Spell Upside For Spear Alpha ETF's (SPRX) Top Holding
feed.summary: Nvidia's stock began trading on a 10-for-1 split-adjusted basis, aiming to make shares more accessible to retail investors after strong Q1 2025 earnings. Spear Alpha ETF (SPRX), with Nvidia as its top holding at 12.88%, may benefit from increased investor access and the broader growth in the AI sector, which is significantly larger than the video-gaming sector that previously drove Nvidia's growth. Despite a recent consolidation phase in Nvidia's stock, its overall technical picture remains bullish given its year-to-date and 52-week performance.

feed.title: Five thoughts from Cisco Live 2024
feed.summary: Cisco Live 2024 showcased the company's aggressive push into AI and the integration of Splunk across its portfolio. Key takeaways include Splunk's expansion beyond security and observability, Cisco's emerging comprehensive AI infrastructure story, significant advancements in its security offerings, and the growing importance of ThousandEyes for network visibility. The event highlighted Cisco's transition towards a cloud-centric strategy and the need for more cross-cloud solutions to create integrated customer experiences.

feed.title: South Korean AI chip developers Rebellions and Sapeon to merge
feed.summary: South Korean AI chip developers Rebellions Inc and Sapeon Korea Inc are merging to challenge global AI chip leader Nvidia. The consolidation, driven by Sapeon parent SK Telecom, aims to secure a strong position in the global AI semiconductor market within the next two to three years, with the merged company expected to launch by the end of the year. Rebellions will manage the merged entity, focusing on neural processing units (NPUs).

feed.title: Archaea Energy and Republic Services celebrate first RNG plant in landmark partnership
feed.summary: Archaea Energy, a bp subsidiary, and Republic Services have launched their first renewable natural gas (RNG) plant in Fort Wayne, Indiana, as part of their Lightning Renewables joint venture. This facility, utilizing Archaea Modular Design (AMD), is the first of approximately 40 planned landfill gas-to-RNG projects aiming to convert landfill gas into sustainable energy. Once operational, the Fort Wayne plant is expected to process enough gas to heat over 25,000 homes annually, significantly contributing to both companies' sustainability goals and increasing domestic energy production.
